evidences.title: Bitcoin (BTC) Price Analysis: November 27, 2025 - Binance
evidences.content: Bitcoin's dominance stands at 59%, down from 61.4% earlier in November, hinting at early capital rotation toward altcoins like Ethereum (+2.9% to $3,039) and Solana (+2.8% to $143). Stablecoin supply remains elevated at ~$308B, with USDC inflows spiking, providing sidelined liquidity for potential risk-on moves post-Thanksgiving. #### Recent Performance and Key Trends [...] Open interest has flushed -9% to ~683,000 BTC, cleaning out leverage from the $80,000 capitulation low on November 21. Funding rates flipped negative (-0.006%), a rare bottoming signal where shorts pay longs – sustained negativity has preceded rallies in 80% of similar instances since 2023. Mid-tier whales (100+ BTC holders) added 91 new entities since November 11, pointing to opportunistic accumulation. On the macro front, today's U.S. data (Core PCE at 0.3% MoM, GDP revision, and jobless claims at 225K) eased rate-cut fears, with markets now pricing a 62% chance of a December Fed cut (down from 82%). This supports a "soft landing" narrative, though holiday liquidity (average returns ~1.5% since 2013) could amplify wicks. #### Technical Analysis [...] #### Technical Analysis BTC is forming a potential bottom...

evidences.title: Binance Historical 2017-2025 (all USDT pairs)
evidences.content: ## About Dataset This dataset contains historical cryptocurrency market data extracted from public Binance API endpoints. The files provided include 1-minute (1m) candlestick (kline) data for all USDT trading pairs, downloaded and updated programmatically from 2017 to 2024. Data is stored in efficient Parquet format for compatibility and performance, covering open, high, low, close, and volume for each time interval. This dataset is ideal for quantitative research, backtesting trading strategies, machine learning projects, and market analysis. Source: (public API, no account required) Updated: Regularly (monthly and daily backfill). Columns: timestamp, open, high, low, close, volume, symbol, and more. evidences.content: ## Dataset Overview This dataset contains historical price data for Bitcoin (BTC/USDT) from January 1, 2018, to the present. evidences.title: Historical Market Data - Binance.US
evidences.content: Historical Market Data We've made Candlestick, Trade, and AggTrade data for all pairs available for download. What Data Is Available We provide data at daily and monthly intervals. Data for a day is uploaded the following day, and we begin to upload data for a month at the beginning of the following month. Data Products Open API ## Frequently Asked Questions Candlestick Also known as k-line, is distinguished by its open time and supports multiple intervals for analysis and interpretation. evidences.title: General API Information | Binance Open Platform
evidences.content: All time and timestamp related fields in the JSON responses are in milliseconds by default. To receive the information in microseconds, please add the header `X-MBX-TIME-UNIT:MICROSECOND` or `X-MBX-TIME-UNIT:microsecond`. We support HMAC, RSA, and Ed25519 keys. For more information, please see API Key types. Timestamp parameters (e.g. `startTime`, `endTime`, `timestamp`) can be passed in milliseconds or microseconds. For APIs that only send public market data, please use the base endpoint Please refer to Market Data Only page. If there are enums or terms you want clarification on, please see the SPOT Glossary for more information. [...] should give better performance but have less stability. Responses are in JSON by default. To receive responses in SBE, refer to the SBE FAQ page. If your request contains a symbol name containing non-ASCII characters, then the response may contain non-ASCII characters encoded in UTF-8. Some endpoints may return asset and/or symbol names containing non-ASCII characters encoded in UTF-8 even if the request did not contain non-ASCII characters. Data is returned in chronological order, unless noted otherwise. Without `startTime` or `endTime`, returns...

evidences.title: Introducing new API updates: Query transaction data for USD ...
evidences.content: To query transaction information for historical trades and orders, involving USD trading pairs, executed before the planned system upgrade, use the USD4 symbol. For example: "BTCUSD4". To query transaction information for trades and orders, involving USD trading pairs, executed after the planned system upgrade, continue using the USD symbol. For example: “BTCUSD”. For more information, please refer to our API changelog.
